T10.pattern_text:
PART 1: CURRENT STATE
Welcome. I'm Unity. You're looking at your February baseline energy field for T10. The chart shows minute-by-minute data across the full month — dark blue represents total kVA supplied by your transformers, light blue is actual kW consumed by your processes, green shows reactive kVAR waste, and orange traces your power factor. You've got a clear food processing rhythm here — strong weekday production blocks running consistently between one thousand and fourteen hundred kVA, dropping to baseline levels around six hundred kVA on weekends. Most concerning is that your weekday operations are running well above optimal efficiency. At 88.8% power factor, you're seeing reactive spikes up to 752 kVAR during startup sequences. That electromagnetic waste is generating over four hundred thousand BTU per hour of thermal burden, requiring 82 kilowatts just for cooling overhead. Bottom line — this field is costing you over forty-six thousand monthly in pure waste. Now let me show you how we clean this up.

PART 2: MPTS SOLUTION
Alright — now we move from observation to correction. Your peak reactive demand hits 752 kVAR during heavy production periods. Take that kVAR MAX, divide by four hundred, round up — that gives us two H490 units delivering 800 kVAR of total reactive compensation for four hundred thousand installed. Here's what MPTS does in plain language — it injects precisely calibrated reactive power to neutralize your inductive loads, bringing power factor to 99.5%. Think of it as electromagnetic tuning. Instead of your transformers supplying both real work power and wasteful reactive power, they focus purely on productive demand. Visually, you'll see that green reactive layer collapse dramatically, conductors run cooler, and you free up substantial capacity in your distribution system. Now let's move into what you should expect to see.

PART 3: EXPECTED RESULTS
Now look at the post-MPTS field. This is what harmonized looks like. The green reactive layer has nearly disappeared — you're seeing total field height drop significantly while your actual consumed power stays identical. Those spikes that were hitting fifteen hundred kVA are now contained much closer to your actual kilowatt demand. Power factor jumps from 88.8% to 99.5%. The electromagnetic waste that was costing over forty-six thousand monthly? Recovered. That's over half a million annually back in your operating budget. Four hundred thousand dollar installation divided by forty-six thousand monthly savings gives you roughly a twelve-month payback period. After that breakeven point, every hour is recovered value flowing directly to your bottom line. Production stays identical — efficiency improves dramatically. That's electromagnetic stewardship — and that's the Unity standard.

T12.pattern_text:
PART 1: CURRENT STATE
Welcome. I'm Unity. You're looking at your February baseline energy field for transformer T12. Let me walk you through these colors. The dark blue shows your total kVA supplied — that's what your conductors actually carry. The light blue beneath is your consumed kW — the work getting done. That green layer? That's your reactive kVAR waste. And the orange line tracks your power factor as it fluctuates. Now look at this production rhythm. Four distinct weekday blocks rising to sixteen hundred, seventeen hundred kVA supplied. Classic food processing pattern. Your refrigeration systems, motor loads — they're all demanding reactive power during production. Weekends drop dramatically to maybe four hundred kVA. Out of roughly twenty-eight days, I'm seeing eighteen to twenty weekdays operating above your baseline target. That puts you at about seventy to seventy-five percent Electric Operations. Here's what that waste looks like: over one thousand kVAR at peak, seven hundred sixty thousand BTU per hour thermal burden, thirty dollars an hour just cooling your electrical infrastructure. Monthly cost: over seventy-three thousand dollars. Now let me show you how we clean this up.

PART 2: MPTS SOLUTION
Alright — now we move from observation to correction. Your peak reactive demand hits one thousand seven kVAR. That's substantial. Here's the sizing math: one thousand seven divided by four hundred equals two point five, round up to three Unity H490 units. Three times four hundred gives us twelve hundred kVAR total capacity. Six hundred thousand dollars installed. Now here's what happens when those MPTS units go live. They inject precisely compensating reactive power — equal and opposite to what your loads demand. Your power factor jumps from eighty-four percent to ninety-nine point five percent. The magnetic field harmonizes. What you'll see visually: that green reactive layer collapses almost entirely. Your conductors run cooler because they're carrying less current to deliver the same work. Your transformer capacity gets freed up. The field becomes clean, efficient, optimized. Now let's move into what you should expect to see.

PART 3: EXPECTED RESULTS
Now look at the post-MPTS field. This is what harmonized looks like. That green reactive layer — virtually gone. Your supplied kVA drops dramatically closer to your consumed kW during production periods. Power factor holds steady at ninety-nine point five percent across all operating conditions. The transformation is complete. Your monthly savings: seventy-three thousand dollars. Annually, that's eight hundred seventy-four thousand recovered. Investment: six hundred thousand installed. Simple math: six hundred thousand divided by seventy-three thousand per month equals eight months payback. After eight months, every single hour is recovered value. No more reactive losses heating your conductors. No more thermal burden taxing your cooling systems. No more capacity constraints limiting your operations. Pure electromagnetic efficiency delivering measurable financial return. That's electromagnetic stewardship — and that's the Unity standard.

Your power factor sits at eighty-four percent. That reactive load is hitting over one thousand kVAR at peak. The numbers are stark: eight hundred seventy-four thousand dollars annually in electromagnetic waste. Your field shows classic food processing demand — those weekday production blocks rising well above baseline, then dropping to minimal weekend loads. After that, every hour is recovered value flowing straight to your bottom line. That's electromagnetic stewardship — and that's the Unity standard.

T15.pattern_text:
PART 1: CURRENT STATE
Welcome. I'm Unity. You're looking at your February baseline energy field for T15. The dark blue layer is your total kVA supply — what the utility delivers. The light blue underneath is your actual kW consumption — the work you're paying for. That thick green layer between them? That's your reactive kVAR waste — electromagnetic burden with no productive value. The orange trace shows your power factor dropping to 80-85% during production peaks. Your field shows classic food processing operations — four distinct weekday production blocks with clean weekend valleys. Eighty-five percent of your operating days push above that 1750 kVA target line. You're carrying 670 thousand BTU per hour of thermal burden, cooling costs of 40 dollars per hour, and total electromagnetic waste of 872 thousand annually. Now let me show you how we clean this up.

PART 2: MPTS SOLUTION
Alright — now we move from observation to correction. Your reactive demand peaks at 829 kVAR. Take that maximum, divide by 400, round up — you need three H490 units. Three times 400 gives you 1200 kVAR of harmonic correction capacity, installed cost 600 thousand dollars. What MPTS does is inject precisely tuned reactive power to cancel your motor loading. Your transformers see clean resistive load, power factor climbs to 99.5%, and that thick green reactive layer collapses to almost nothing. Your conductors run cooler, your capacity frees up, and you deliver the same production work with dramatically less electromagnetic burden. Now let's move into what you should expect to see.

PART 3: EXPECTED RESULTS
Now look at the post-MPTS field. This is what harmonized looks like. That thick green reactive waste layer has been compressed to almost nothing. Your dark blue supply demand now tracks cleanly with your light blue production needs. The orange power factor trace holds steady at 99.5% — true electromagnetic efficiency. Monthly savings: 72 thousand 700 dollars. Annual recovery: 809 thousand dollars. Your install cost of 600 thousand divided by monthly savings gives you eight months to payback. After month eight, every hour is recovered value — over 67 thousand per month flowing straight to your bottom line. That's electromagnetic stewardship — and that's the Unity standard.

T16.pattern_text:
PART 1: CURRENT STATE
Welcome. I'm Unity. You're looking at your February baseline energy field for T16. The dark blue layer shows your total kVA supply — that's what you're drawing from the grid. The light blue beneath it is your actual kW consumed — the work being done. That green reactive layer — that's the waste. The orange line tracks your power factor hovering around 88 percent. You're seeing classic food processing rhythm here. Five distinct weekday production blocks running 1200 to 1500 kVA range, dropping to minimal weekend valleys around 200 to 400 kVA. About 60 to 65 percent of your production time runs above efficient target levels. Peak demand hits 1455 kilowatts, but that reactive component — 697.7 kVAR at peak — is creating 466,524 BTU per hour of thermal burden. That's costing you over $9 per hour in cooling overhead alone, with total waste hitting $464,617 annually. Now let me show you how we clean this up.

PART 2: MPTS SOLUTION
Alright — now we move from observation to correction. Your peak reactive demand is 697.7 kVAR. Take that, divide by 400, round up — that's two H490 units you need. Two units deliver 800 kVAR of compensating capacity for $200,000 installed. Here's what MPTS does in plain language: it injects precisely timed reactive current that cancels your inductive load demands. Your power factor jumps to 99.5 percent. Conductors run cooler. Transformer capacity gets freed up. Visually, you'll see that green reactive layer collapse down to almost nothing — your apparent power drops toward your real power demand. Same production output, dramatically less electromagnetic waste. Now let's move into what you should expect to see.

PART 3: EXPECTED RESULTS
Now look at the post-MPTS field. This is what harmonized looks like. That green reactive layer has virtually disappeared — you're seeing clean power delivery where your consumed power closely matches your total supply envelope. Power factor locked at 99.5 percent. Those weekday production blocks now run tight and efficient. The savings: $440,027 recovered annually. That's a $200,000 installation recovering $36,669 monthly — under six months payback. After that, every hour is recovered value. Your cooling costs drop. Your thermal burden disappears. Your electrical capacity gets freed for growth instead of waste. That's electromagnetic stewardship — and that's the Unity standard.
